_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**Carrier Qualification and Vetting Systems — Immediate Gap**
The workspace's current subcarrier qualification process likely references federal GHG standards (EPA MY-year certifications) as a proxy for emissions compliance. With those standards repealed at the federal level and CARB now operating on a reinstated legacy standard baseline, the workspace must update its carrier qualification questionnaire and onboarding checklist to capture CARB-specific certification vintage and standard applicability. Scope: all road carriers operating in California and in Section 177 states. Deadline: 30 days. Gap from baseline: moderate — existing federal GHG reference fields must be replaced or supplemented with CARB legacy certification fields.

**Contract Clause Review — Emissions Compliance Representations**
Standard freight forwarding subcontracting agreements frequently include representations that carriers will comply with 'applicable federal and state emissions standards.' Given the current legal uncertainty — federal standards repealed, state standards in litigation, Section 177 state enforcement postures diverging — boilerplate emissions compliance clauses now create ambiguous risk allocation. The workspace should work with legal counsel to revise these clauses to (a) specify the operative CARB standard vintage for California hauls, (b) require carrier notification within 5 business days of any change in CARB certification status, and (c) include a regulatory-change adaptation clause tied to D.C. Circuit litigation outcomes. Deadline: 60 days or prior to next contract renewal cycle.

**Regulatory Change Monitoring Infrastructure**
The pace of rulemaking and litigation in this docket — EPA proposed rule (July 2025), public hearings (August 2025), final rule (February 2026), state lawsuit (March 2026), CARB Emergency Regulation (March 2026) — demonstrates that material compliance requirements can shift within weeks. The workspace should establish a dedicated monitoring feed for: (1) D.C. Circuit docket in the California et al. v. EPA Endangerment Finding case; (2) CARB regulatory notices; (3) Federal Register entries for companion EPA GHG rulemakings. Scope: regulatory intelligence function or third-party subscription. Deadline: immediate.

**Supplier/Carrier Training and Communication**
Carriers serving the workspace's California-centric cargo verticals (live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive — all with significant LA/SF activity) should receive written communication clarifying that the workspace requires CARB legacy-standard certification documentation regardless of federal repeal. This protects the workspace from procuring non-compliant carriers in a state where enforcement remains active. Scope: all California-route carriers. Deadline: 30 days.

**Scenario Planning for D.C. Circuit Injunction**
If the D.C. Circuit issues a preliminary injunction reinstating federal GHG standards pending litigation, the workspace's carriers could face dual compliance obligations on short notice. The workspace should maintain a dual-compliance scenario document identifying which carriers in its network are: (a) certified to both legacy CARB and formerly-applicable federal GHG standards, (b) certified to legacy CARB only, (c) certified to federal GHG standards only. This gap analysis enables rapid requalification if federal standards are judicially reinstated. Deadline: 45 days.

*Source: CARB Emergency Vehicle Emissions Regulation, CARB, March 26, 2026. https://ww2.arb.ca.gov/news/carb-approves-permanent-adoption-emergency-vehicle-emissions-regulation; DOJ Lawsuit against CARB, U.S. Department of Justice, August 15, 2025. https://www.justice.gov/opa/pr/justice-department-sues-california-end-enforcement-unlawful-emissions-standards-trucks; Hanson Bridgett Legal Analysis, February 2026. https://www.hansonbridgett.com/publication/250226-2340-clean-air-act-waiver*
